summary: DHH highlights a milestone in the early Ruby on Rails community: a developer was hired directly through the #rubyonrails IRC channel. He notes that the Rails job market is growing, with company owners actively hanging out in the IRC channel alongside developers. The post celebrates the informal, community-driven hiring that characterized the early Rails ecosystem.
key_points: The Ruby on Rails job market was already heating up in late 2004, shortly after the framework's release
DHH was personally connecting companies with Rails developers, acting as a matchmaker for the nascent ecosystem
Company owners were hanging out in the #rubyonrails IRC channel, blurring the line between community participation and recruiting
A developer was hired as a freelancer without ever meeting the employer in person, foreshadowing remote-first culture
money_quotes: The job market for Ruby on Rails is heating up.
I've been hooking up companies with developers left and right.
Even the #rubyonrails IRC channel has turned into a potential job interview.
We got quite a few firm owners hanging out and one of them just hired a developer off the channel.
key_insight: The early Rails community was so tight-knit and fast-growing that its IRC channel doubled as both a technical forum and an organic hiring marketplace.
